answer:
The limit of \( I(R) \) as \( R \to \infty \) is the integral over the entire plane of the given difference of functions. The first term integrates to \(\pi\) and the second to \(\pi/2\), so the result is \(\pi - \pi/2 = \pi/2 \approx 1.5708\). Among the options, the closest is 1.53978589.

\boxed{G}
